negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing txpool maintenance that reads CHAIN_HALT_THRESHOLD_SECS with default 10, compares latest-block wall-clock divergence, and evicts all scheduled transactions as poisonous only when threshold is exceeded.
related_but_insufficient_code: The port preserves saturating scheduled max-block arithmetic, but maintain.rs explicitly omits the old chain-halt scheduled tx eviction loop.

notes: Confirmed gap because one required half of the invariant is explicitly absent.
rationale: These are separate defensive arithmetic/maintenance rules around scheduled state: chain halt eviction is operator-configurable, and legacy event bounds are derived with saturating arithmetic.
statement: Transaction-pool maintenance must evict scheduled transactions as poisonous only when wall-clock divergence from the latest block exceeds CHAIN_HALT_THRESHOLD_SECS, defaulting to 10 on absent or invalid configuration, and Scheduler event parsers must derive legacy max-block fields with saturating last-execution arithmetic.
suggested_falsification_tests: Run maintenance with scheduled transactions and controlled timestamps; assert eviction honors unset, invalid, and valid CHAIN_HALT_THRESHOLD_SECS values.

Decode cancelled, executed, completed, and failed events with startBlock/frequency/numCalls values that would overflow unchecked arithmetic and assert parsing succeeds with saturated legacy max block.
summary: Scheduled maintenance/event defensive bounds are only partially present; chain-halt scheduled eviction behavior is absent.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Run txpool maintenance with raw scheduled tx and stale latest block timestamp >10 seconds; expected poisonous eviction path is absent.
title: Scheduled maintenance and event bounds are defensive
